# Layoff Check Entry

The Layoff Check Entry screen is used any time you want to
 calculate a paycheck outside of the regular pay cycle.
The Layoff Check Entry screen is similar to the
 Time Card Entry screens.
When time card information is entered, the software will then calculate taxes, dues,
 deductions and add-ons, subject-to wages, and the final check dollar amount using the work
 date specified on the main screen. For any time card line that has no work date, the period
 end date specified in the heading section of the main screen is used. With this
 information, a manual check can be written.
Data
 entered through this screen is for informational purposes only, and does not automatically
 update to employee records. If necessary, the information can be transferred to the Pre-Time Card
 Entry screen and from there used in a regular pay cycle as a manual check. When
 doing this, the deductions are saved for the manual check, so the
 figures won't need to be re-entered during Payroll Adjustment Entry.
Check Calculation includes calculations based on the Payroll formula
 variables that are available in the Payroll > Formula File Maintenance screen.
This screen consists of two parts, which display in succession:
 Layoff Check Entry and Layoff Check
 Display. When Time Card Entry is complete, select
 OK. Calculations then display on the [Layoff Check Display](/en/spectrum/spectrum/accounting/payroll/data-entry/layoff-check-display).
The screen also has an option for immediately typing an instant check
 onto the standard payroll check stock before exiting the screen. Select the New
 button to open the [Layoff Check Entry
 window](/en/spectrum/spectrum/accounting/payroll/data-entry/layoff-check-entry/layoff-check-entry-window) to add new layoff check lines.
[Auto-Signature manual](https://trimble.deploy.heretto.com/v4/deployments/mVyHSMAo9PbkfqcxUBWF/object/0751eeb0-8ea9-11ec-9179-02420ae60f1a?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..p4JGvKzvKWYo1bD3BjfUhQ8vHi36QVlmyFSc6z43fZw&response-content-disposition=filename%3D%22Auto-Signature_Procedures_v1422.pdf%22)
